According to the survey, 24% of CEOs are “extremely concerned” about climate-related issues, compared to 38% for over-regulation. As they gather for the World Economic Forum, CEOs and politicians like U.S. President Donald Trump are set to face mounting pressure from environmental groups and activists like Swedish teen Greta Thunberg to respond to the climate emergency. The meeting follows last week’s revelation that the last decade was the hottest ever recorded on Earth. The survey also found that the number of CEOs who are pessimistic about the economic outlook has almost doubled over the past year, with 53% predicting a decline in the rate of growth this year, up from 29% in 2019. texts.full_text: of bleeding with conservative treatment, we opted for a operative intervention. Although Liver Transplant (LT) eliminates most of the varices due to the reconstruction of the liver anatomy, esophagogastric varices may persist or newly develop after LT due to hepatic vein outflow obstruction or spontaneous portal-to-systemic shunts. We performed a laparoscope-assisted Hassab's operation, which involves the dissection and division of the short gastric vessels to relieve tension on the gastric wall and reduce the varices. The operation was successful, with no intra- or postoperative bleeding, and the patient was discharged on postoperative day 7. However, unfortunately, the patient was readmitted with upper gastrointestinal bleeding 20 months after the operation. Repeat esophagogastroduodenoscopy showed recurrence of the esophagogastric varices with an enlarged loci superior (Ls) and moderator, and large bead-like varices (F3). Despite endoscopic injection sclerotherapy and rubber band ligation, he experienced persistent bleeding, and therefore, a left gastric artery embolization was performed. This case report highlights the importance of close follow-up of patients after LDLT for the early detection and management of esophagogastric varices, which can remain a significant cause of bleeding despite transplantation. texts.full_text: ith interest to that date. In its income tax return for the calendar year 1936, the plaintiff claimed a deduction of the amount of interest paid the Reconstruction Finance Corporation on the $100,000 loan in the sum of $5,000. The Commissioner disallowed the deduction and assessed a deficiency against the plaintiff in the sum of $2,593.43. The question for our determination is whether the interest paid by the plaintiff to the Reconstruction Finance Corporation was a deductible item under the provisions of the Revenue Act of 1936, 50 Stat. 988, as amended, 26 U.S.C.A. § 23. The plaintiff contends that the Reconstruction Finance Corporation was in effect the source of the funds with which it made the loan, and that the plaintiff was therefore entitled to a deduction under section 23 of the Revenue Act of 1936. The Reconstruction Finance Corporation was created by Congress for the purpose of aiding banks in their rehabilitation, and, to that end, was given broad powers, including the power to make loans to banks, and to purchase and sell obligations of banks. 12 U.S.C.A. §§ 226, 227. The Reconstruction Finance Corporation is not a corporation owned by the Government, but is a corporation owned by the shareholders thereof. The Reconstruction Finance Corporation did not loan the plaintiff any funds, nor did it have any funds of its to loan to the plaintiff. It made a loan to the bank, and the bank made a loan to the plaintiff. The Reconstruction Finance Corporation is not a agent of the Government, but is an corporation owned by shareholders, and it was the plaintiff's contention that the Reconstruction Finance Corporation was, in effect, the source of the funds with which the plaintiff made the loan. The plaintiff relies on the following cases: The United States v. American National Bank & Trust Co., 326 U.S. 605, 66 S.Ct. 345, 90 L.Ed. 385; Johnson v. texts.full_text: Sen. Amy Klobuchar is buying some ad time on at least one Twin Cities TV station. WCCO-TV’s political file reports that Klobuchar has reserved roughly $80,000 in ads from Oct. 1 through Oct. 8. The ad buy, which was first reported by The Associated Press, comes as Klobuchar is polling well ahead of Republican Kurt Bills. A spokesman for Klobuchar would only say that it’s the first portion of a statewide ad buy and that it’s the first ad the campaign is running this election cycle. Bills’ campaign has not formally inquired about purchasing ad time at the four major Twin Cities TV stations. He is at a significant financial disadvantage heading into the November election. Klobuchar reported having more than $5 million in the bank in July. The Bills campaign had roughly $5,000 in the bank. Bills, however, issued a statement criticizing Klobuchar for campaigning in Iowa on Tuesday for congressional candidate Christie Vilsack. The Minnesota U.S. Senate race between Senator Amy Klobuchar (DFL) and Republican Kurt Bills is heating up, with Klobuchar taking an early lead in advertising spending. Here's a breakdown of the key points: 1. Klobuchar's Ad Campaign: She has reserved $80,000 for ads on Twin Cities TV stations from October 1-8, marking her first ad push in this election cycle. (if i developed my front-end app in Web-tier and back-end in Worker-tier) A: The most important differencein my opinion is that worker tier instances do not run web server processes(apache, nginx, etc). As such, they do not directly respond to customer requests. Instead, t...theyare designed to handle long-running tasks, such as video processing, image resizing, orother compute-intensive operations. Thesetasks can be triggered byyour web application, but theyare executed independently of the web request-response cycle. On the other hand, web-tier instances are designed to run web server processes and directly respond to customer requests. They are responsible for servingweb content, handling HTTP requests, and providing a responsive userexperience. In terms of services available, both tiers have access to AWS services such as Amazon RDS, Amazon S3, and Amazon SQS. However, the web-tier hasadditional servicesavailable,such as Elastic Load Balancer (ELB) and Amazon Route 53, which are essential for handling web traffic. Regarding communication between the two tiers, they can communicate with each other using messaging services like AmazonSQSor Amazon SNS. texts.full_text: tosh https://techcrunch.com/2020/07/19/the-dual-phd-problem-of-todays-startups/ ====== woeirua The reason you don't see more startups in the hard sciences is not due to the lack of hybrid talent as this article surmises. It's because: 1 - VCs are reluctant to fund capital intensive startups that have time horizons for exits that are significantly longer than software based startups. 2 - The product lifecycle is so much longer, which makes it inherently much riskier. In many cases it can be years before you even get to the point wheryou can start generating revenue, and even longer before you can achieve profitability or a significant exit. This extended timeline can be a deterrent for investors who prefer shorter timeframes for returns. 3 - The regulatory environment for hard science startups is often more complex and stringent, especially in fields like biotech, pharmaceuticals, and medical devices. Navigating these regulations can be costly and time-consuming, adding another layer of risk and complexity to the startup journey. 4 - The market for hard science innovations can be more fragmented and less predictable compared to software solutions. While software can often be scaled rapidly and adopted widely, hard science products may require more time to gain traction and acceptance in specific markets. 5 - The talent pool for hard science startups is smaller and more specialized, which can make it challenging to build a strong team with the necessary expertise. This scarcity of talent can drive up costs and make it more difficult to find the right people to bring a product to market. 6 - Finally, the failure rate for hard science startups is often higher due to the inherent risks and uncertainties associated with developing and commercializing new technologies. This higher failure rate can deter investors and entrepreneurs from pursuing opportunities in these fields. These factors combined contribute to the challenges faced by hard science startups, rather than the presence or

No matter how much pain and suffering there is around us, no amount of it will ever diminish God's love for you personally; nor does any sin or failure disqualify anyone entirely out-of bounds beyond redemption! Even if sometimes we feel unlovable ourselves due to past mistakes made along life’s journey thus far - even then still stands true: "God is Love," says St John in his first letter (4th chapter). And love doesn't change based on circumstances or conditions outside itself. It just...is! Unwavering, steadfast and constant through every upheaval imaginable – be it joyous celebration at weddings & births OR devastating loss during funerals/tragedies alike… This unchanging nature provides a foundation upon which we can build our lives knowing that whatever happens tomorrow (or even today!), there remains an anchor point holding firm against life's stormy seas. So let us strive daily towards living out these principles outlined earlier, not merely because they sound nice but rather BECAUSE WE ARE LOVED BY GOD WHO NEVER CHANGES!! DEEDS OF LOVE In the midst of oppressive poverty, crime, and woundedness—children and youth can reach the false conclusion they are less lovable, acceptable, or capable than others. Then hope in this world and the next fades. So Seek and Save aims to pair the words of God’s love for them with acts of love to them. Thus, the Gospel of salvation through faith in Jesus is presented as living hope because “faith by itself, if it is not accompanied by action, is dead” (James 2:17). Our goal is for these actions of love to be motivated and performed according to divine standards of love (1 Cor. 13:1-13) in an authentic relationship. Seek & Save is dedicated to the urban young facing a lack of resources, experiences, and opportunities. And we serve those who have suffered from neglect, abuse, and exploitation. Reaching out to them includes going to under-resourced schools, homes in economically-challenged neighborhoods, community-based organizations serving at-risk and exploited youth, juvenile detention facilities, foster homes, and shelters. 5

texts.full_text: Review article: Potential cellular therapies for renal disease: can we translate results from animal studies to the human condition? The incidence of chronic kidney disease is increasing worldwide, prompting considerable research into potential regenerative therapies. These have included studies to determine whether an endogenous renal stem cell exists in the postnatal kidney and whether non-renal adult stem cells, such as mesenchymal stem cell, can ameliorate renal damage. Such stem cells will either need to be recruited to the damaged kidney to repair the damaged tissue or alternatively, they could be expanded ex vivo and then administered to the patient. texts.full_text: st Fleet: Dauntless The Lost Fleet: Dauntless is a science fiction novel by American writer Jack Campbell, the first in his The Lost Fleet series, published in 2006. Dauntless sets the stage for the six novel saga about a fleet of over 200 ships trapped deep behind enemy lines and cut off from traveling to their home territory. Plot summary John 'Black Jack' Geary has recently been rescued from a 100-year-old escape pod with a damaged beacon. He was the commanding officer of an early battle in what has become a century-old war between the Syndicate Worlds and the Alliance. His last actions in that battle led to his immortalization as a hero of the Alliance people and fleet, which by the time of the book has become blown out of proportion. Still feeling weak from being frozen for 100 years, Geary arrives at what is supposed to be a decisive battle for the Alliance against the Syndicate. The battle turns out to be a trap and as the leaders of the fleet board a shuttle to negotiate surrender, the Admiral calls on Geary to lead the fleet if anything should happen to him. Geary, assuming that the old laws of war still apply and that nothing ill would happen to his leaders, accepts. Mere hours later after the Admiral is executed, he finds himself in command of 200 ships that have been badly beaten and are cut off from retreat. texts.full_text: Dean, Wiltshire West Dean is a village, civil parish and former manor in Wiltshire (historically in Hampshire), England, situated on the Wiltshire/Hampshire border. The village is on the River Dun, about east of Salisbury. History A Roman villa site straddles the present-day Wiltshire/Hampshire border. The village was mentioned in the Cartularium Saxonicum for the year 880 as (æt) Deone, and may have formed part of the inheritance of Aethelweard, youngest son of King Alfred. It is mentioned as Duene in the Domesday Book of 1086 (one of the 51 holdings of Waleran the Hunter), as Westdone in 1265, and as Westdune in 1270. Dean House, which straddles the border with Hampshire, is a Grade II* listed former rectory from the late 17th century, enlarDean House, which straddles the border with Hampshire, is a Grade II* listed former rectory from the late 17th century, enlarged and remodelled in the 19th century. The house is a significant architectural and historical landmark in the village, with its historical and architectural importance recognized and preserved. The rectory, and the village itself, have a rich and layered history, from the Roman era through the Saxon and Norman periods, and into the modern era.

texts.full_text: independent in the 1960's, ''it suited the islands, scattered as they were, that they could have one court, instead of many, serving separate jurisdictions,'' said Sir Probyn Inniss, a distinguished jurist from St. Kitts and Nevis. ''Because of tradition and the commonwealth countries paradigm, it was the logical thing to do.'' In addition, Sir Probyn said, it was important ''to the people of the Caribbean to have a final court of appeal perceived as being untainted by any partisan political considerations.'' And over the years, the Privy Council has taken a stance that is largely ''more in favor of greater personal freedoms, a wider measure of constitutional rights, and more and more safeguards for the individual citizen,'' he said. But the generation now coming to maturity throughout the Caribbean has no memory of the colonial era, and so puts less value on maintaining traditions and institutions inherited from Britain. In many countries, the Queen and the Privy Council that she appoints are viewed as remote, irrelevant and easily replaceable. ''The Privy Council has no idea what is going on in our societies, and so it should not be making decisions on West Indies cases,'' said Lysanne Simmons, a 22-year-old law student at the University of the West Indies here. ''We should have our own court of last instance, because we are the only ones who know what is going on.'' At the time of independence, ''we needed guidance, but we have experience now, and can stand on our own feet and make our own decisions,'' Ms. Simmons said. ''The old generation came up singing 'God Save the Queen,' but to us, she's just a figurehead, and we don't need those ties.'' In place of the Privy Council, its critics want all 12
